findings.summary: statistics.dnp_parts reports 0 but 8 DNP components are present; pwr_flag_warnings falsely report +5V, +24V Bus, VCC, GND, +3.3V as missing PWR_FLAG; bus_topology width counts label instances inste...

correct.description: All 41 components are correctly identified with accurate references, values, footprints, MPNs, and categories. The 33 non-DNP in-BOM components match the sourcing_audit total of 32 (J6 test point is not in BOM). USB D+/D- differential pair is correctly detected with J1 and U1. CC1/CC2 5.1K pulldown resistors (R3, R4) are correctly identified and the cc1_pulldown_5k1 and cc2_pulldown_5k1 USB compliance checks pass. Voltage dividers (R5/R6 for bus monitoring, R8/R11 for ADC reference) are correctly detected. UART signals (TXD, RXD, BUS_TX) on U1 correctly identified. Q1 (BC817) NPN transistor driving the +24V Bus rail is correctly characterized as a switch-mode circuit.

The warnings correctly flag that SSR, LED_RED, LED_RGB, and BUS_TX have both 'input' and 'output' shaped global labels (inconsistent direction annotations), and that BUS_WIRE1 and BUS_WIRE2 have input-shaped labels but no driver pins — these are inter-board signals routed through the connector J2 without a local driver, which is accurate for this gateway design.

incorrect.description: The schematic has 8 components with (dnp yes): J3 (3V3 Test Pad), J4 (TX Test Pad), J5 (RX Test Pad), J7 (BUS RX Test Pad), C8 (33uF), JP2 (SolderJumper_2_Open), R8 (10K), R11 (10K). All 8 appear correctly in the components list with dnp=true, but statistics.dnp_parts is 0 instead of 8. This is a statistics aggregation bug — the individual component records are correct but the summary count is wrong.

The schematic places PWR_FLAG power symbols co-located with other power symbols at y=185.42: (+5V at x=24.13, PWR_FLAG at x=24.13), (GND at x=46.99, PWR_FLAG at x=46.99), (+3.3V at x=71.12, PWR_FLAG at x=71.12), (VCC at x=35.56, PWR_FLAG at x=35.56), (+24V Bus at x=58.42, PWR_FLAG at x=58.42). These co-located power symbols form connected nets in KiCad, so each of those rails DOES have a PWR_FLAG. The analyzer fails to correlate co-located power symbols into the same wire net, issuing false pwr_flag_warnings for all five rails.

The bus_topology section reports: BUS_WIRE with width=5 (range BUS_WIRE1..BUS_WIRE2), IO with width=4 (range IO40..IO41), RELAY_WIRE with width=4 (range RELAY_WIRE1..RELAY_WIRE2). In each case the width equals the total number of label placement instances rather than the count of unique signal names. BUS_WIRE1 appears 3 times and BUS_WIRE2 appears 2 times on the schematic (5 instances total, 2 unique signals), so width should be 2. IO40 appears 2 times and IO41 appears 2 times (4 instances, 2 unique signals), so width should be 2. RELAY_WIRE1 appears 2 times and RELAY_WIRE2 appears 2 times (4 instances, 2 unique signals), so width should be 2. The detection threshold of 3 label instances also means BUS_WIRE1 alone triggers bus detection for a two-wire bus.

U2 is an AP63203WU (Diodes Inc. 2A 3.3V buck switching regulator, lib_id=Regulator_Switching:AP63203WU). It is surrounded by L1 (3.9uH), C3 (470uF), C4 (10uF), C5 (100nF), and D1/D5/D6 (1N5819WS Schottky diodes) — a textbook switching regulator circuit. However, signal_analysis.power_regulators is empty because the string 'ap63' appears in the _non_reg_exclude list (added in KH-100 to prevent the AP6236 WiFi module from being detected as a regulator). The 'ap63' prefix matches AP63203WU, causing it to be skipped. The exclusion is a known tradeoff from KH-100 but it incorrectly suppresses a legitimate regulator here.

The differential_pairs entry for USB_D+/USB_D- sets has_esd=true and esd_protection=['U1']. The USB_D+ net connects J1 (USB-C receptacle) pin A6/B6 to U1 (ESP32-S3-WROOM-1) pin 14 (IO20). The USB_D- net connects J1 pin A7/B7 to U1 pin 13 (IO19). There is no external ESD diode, TVS diode, or ESD-specific IC (e.g., USBLC6-2SC6, TPD2E009) present on these nets. Classifying U1 itself as ESD protection is incorrect — while the ESP32-S3 has internal ESD cells, it is the load, not an ESD protection device. Notably, usb_compliance also correctly flags vbus_esd_protection=fail, which contradicts the has_esd=true claim in differential_pairs.

The net named 'SSR' connects U1 (ESP32-S3) pin 35 (IO42) to U3 (GAQY252G3S, a PhotoMOS solid-state relay) pin 1. The analyzer classifies SSR as 'chip_select' because its name matches the CS/SS SPI chip-select pattern heuristic. However, SSR stands for Solid State Relay and drives the optical input of a PhotoMOS relay (U3 = GAQY252G3S). It is a digital control/drive output, not a chip select. The classification is misleading for this specific signal, though it is a minor naming-heuristic limitation rather than a hard error.

findings.summary: statistics.dnp_parts reports 0 but actual component DNP status should be checked; bus_topology.detected_bus_signals GPIO width=16 is wrong; only 8 unique GPIO signals present; Power regulator U2 (X...

correct.description: U2 is an XC6210B332MR (700mA LDO, lib_id=Regulator_Linear:XC6210B332MR). The signal_analysis.power_regulators correctly identifies it with topology='LDO', input_rail='__unnamed_1', output_rail='+3.3V'. The associated L1 (TBD inductor) connects between +5V and the unnamed VIN net — the LDO is correctly modeled as using the filtered supply. The XC6210 is correctly classified as LDO not switching despite having an inductor on its input side.

The signal_analysis.voltage_dividers correctly identifies R7 (1M) as top and R8 (160K) as bottom with top_net=TASTA_ONLY_BUS, mid_net=GPIO26, bottom_net=GND, ratio=0.138. The mid-point connections to U3 (ESP32-PICO-MINI-02) pin IO26 and TP7 are correctly captured.

incorrect.description: The doorman_slot schematic has no (dnp yes) instances confirmed by source grep. The statistics.dnp_parts=0 is correct for this file. However, the statistics.total_components=36 includes all 10 test point components even though they have in_bom=no. The BOM has 26 components (non-test-point, non-DNP), while sourcing_audit.total_bom_components=26 is consistent.

The bus_topology reports GPIO with width=16 and range GPIO0..GPIO27 with 20 signals listed as missing. The 16 label instances across GPIO0(x2), GPIO4(x2), GPIO12(x2), GPIO13(x2), GPIO14(x2), GPIO15(x2), GPIO26(x2), GPIO27(x2) represent only 8 unique GPIO signals. The width=16 is the raw label instance count, not the unique signal count. The range GPIO0..GPIO27 is also misleading since only 8 of the 28 possible GPIO numbers are actually used. The threshold of >=3 instances to qualify as a bus also means this triggers because GPIO14 appears twice (2 instances each for 8 signals = 16 total).

The doorman_slot schematic's PWR_FLAG symbols are not visible in the extracted power_symbols list. However, the schematic does use PWR_FLAG (it appears in statistics.power_rails). The pwr_flag_warnings flag +5V and GND as lacking PWR_FLAG, which may be a false positive from the same co-located power symbol resolution issue seen in the main doorman schematic. Without full access to the placement coordinates in the slot schematic, the root cause is the same underlying bug: the analyzer cannot correlate co-located power symbols into the same net.

findings.summary: pwr_flag_warnings falsely report +5V, +3.3V, and GND as missing PWR_FLAG; bus_topology GPIO width=4, range GPIO0..GPIO27, but only 2 unique GPIO signals present; Component inventory of 13 component...

correct.description: All 13 components are correctly identified: J1 (Conn_01x06_Socket), J2 and J3 (70AAJ-4-M0 Bourns modular contacts with correct Mouser PN 652-70AAJ-4-M0), and 10 test points (TP1-TP10) all with in_bom=true as specified in the schematic instances. The single no-connect at J1 is correctly counted. Power rails +3.3V, +5V, GND, and PWR_FLAG are correctly identified.

The design_analysis.bus_analysis.uart correctly identifies TX and RX nets as UART signals with pin_count=3 (test point, connector pin, and a second connector pin each), reflecting the breakout nature of this programmer board.

incorrect.description: Three PWR_FLAG symbols at (x=196.85, y=173.99), (x=208.28, y=173.99), (x=185.42, y=173.99) are co-located exactly with +5V at (196.85, 173.99), GND at (208.28, 173.99), and +3.3V at (185.42, 173.99). In KiCad, co-located power symbols connect to the same net, so +5V, GND, and +3.3V each have a PWR_FLAG. The pwr_flag_warnings erroneously flags all three rails as lacking PWR_FLAG. This is the same co-located-power-symbol resolution bug as in the main doorman schematic.

The programmer schematic has GPIO0 appearing 3 times and GPIO27 appearing once (4 label instances total). The bus_topology reports width=4 and range GPIO0..GPIO27 with 26 signals listed as missing. The correct unique signal count is 2 (GPIO0 and GPIO27). Additionally, GPIO27 alone would not normally constitute a 'bus' — the detection is triggered solely by the 3 duplicate instances of GPIO0 meeting the >=3 threshold. This is the same label-instance-counting bug as in the other two schematics.
